audit_name: Prompt 1 / Prompt 2 completion audit for Phase 3 validation

summary.prompt1: mostly complete, with geometry/topology/transport still fixed or placeholder and ledger reconstruction partial beyond Phi
summary.prompt2: partially integrated; governed local text path is active, but several subsystems remain bypassed or only indirectly integrated

items.notes: Typed blocks exist in hrm_core.state and are used by the canonical transition.
freeze_state creates read-only numpy snapshots and proposals read from them.
The canonical engine is real and exercised by tests, but the full integration path still needed a release validation harness.
Diffusion, reaction, regional, input projection, and bounded field updates are present in the canonical path.
Memory and cognition mechanisms are active and consume executive metadata in the integrated path.
A bounded hierarchy mechanism exists and contributes to canonical transitions.
BudgetState exists and is updated in the canonical transition.
Geometry state exists, but adaptive geometry mechanics are disabled or not meaningfully integrated.
Typed topology event machinery is present only as a disabled no-op in the fixed-graph phase.
Only identity transport is implemented; non-identity transport remains deferred.
The safety layer detects invalid values and bounds field/cognition state with logged corrections.
Ledger reconstruction works for the fixed-carrier slice; block-level attribution remains limited and some structural effects are still represented sparsely.
Deterministic replay is supported for the mock provider path and core tests, but broader replay classes remain pending.
Provider-neutral adapter exists with mock deterministic and Ollama providers.
Executive governance is implemented through typed proposals and response envelopes.
Response rendering exists and consumes governed envelopes rather than raw model output.
Persistent memory systems exist, but unification under the HRM memory block is only partial and some legacy paths still stand alone.
Multimodal tests and pipeline exist, but the HRM substrate is not yet the sole production owner of multimodal state.
Tool requests are wrapped in governed plans and route through the integrated runtime.
Distributed coordinator exists, but it remains a separate execution layer and does not yet own the canonical HRM state path.
Learning components exist, but promotion/rollback is not yet fully tied into the governed live HRM transition path.
The integrated runtime supports local-first operation with mock deterministic fallback.
No concrete hardware-profile system or selection logic is present in the repository.
Checkpoint creation exists, but robust crash recovery and checkpoint migration are not yet implemented.
A CLI entrypoint exists via hrm.runner, but a coherent validation/API surface is not yet complete.
Metrics, checkpoints, and result traces exist, but end-to-end structured observability is not yet complete.
Spatial reconstruction, sequence memory, multimodal, distributed, and learning-related tests/experiments are present.
Core ablations exist for some field mechanisms, but the full validation matrix is not yet present.
No deployment scripts, containers, or release packaging scripts were found.
Core docs are present and accurate for the substrate, but validation/release documentation is still incomplete.
